title: Poponax schaffneri
taxonomy: Poponax schaffneri is a plant species that belongs to the family Asteraceae. It is a member of the genus Poponax, which is a group of flowering plants native to the Americas. The classification of Poponax schaffneri is as follows: kingdom: Plantae, clade: Angiosperms, clade: Eudicots, and order: Asterales. The family Asteraceae is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as the sunflower or daisy family. Poponax schaffneri is a specific species within this family and genus.
uses: Poponax schaffneri has been used in traditional medicine, particularly in Mexico, for its potential benefits in treating various ailments. Traditionally, the plant is used to treat fever, inflammation, and infections. Its medicinal properties are attributed to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones and other compounds that exhibit antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory activities. While more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness, the plant has been used in folk medicine for centuries.
